# Vite

Vite is a next-generation frontend build tool that provides a faster and leaner development experience for modern web projects. It consists of two major parts: a dev server that serves source files over native ES modules with extremely fast Hot Module Replacement (HMR), and a build command that bundles your code with Rolldown for highly optimized static assets. Vite supports TypeScript, JSX, CSS preprocessors, and static asset handling out of the box.

Vite is highly extensible through its Plugin API and JavaScript API, with full TypeScript support. It offers sensible defaults while allowing extensive customization for different frameworks and build requirements. The tool is framework-agnostic with official plugins for Vue, React, Preact, and other major frameworks.

## Getting Started

Create a new Vite project using the scaffolding tool with interactive prompts for template selection.

```bash
# Using npm
npm create vite@latest

# Using npm with specific project name and template
npm create vite@latest my-vue-app -- --template vue

# Using pnpm
pnpm create vite

# Using yarn
yarn create vite

# Using bun
bun create vite
```

## Manual Installation

Install Vite as a dev dependency in an existing project.

```bash
# Install vite
npm install -D vite

# Create index.html entry point
echo '<script type="module" src="/src/main.js"></script>' > index.html

# Start dev server
npx vite

# Build for production
npx vite build

# Preview production build locally
npx vite preview
```

## CLI Commands

Vite provides CLI commands for development, building, and previewing applications.

```bash
# Start dev server (aliases: vite dev, vite serve)
vite [root]
vite --host 0.0.0.0 --port 3000
vite --open                          # Open browser on startup
vite --force                         # Force re-bundle dependencies

# Build for production
vite build [root]
vite build --outDir build            # Custom output directory
vite build --sourcemap               # Generate source maps
vite build --minify terser           # Use terser for minification
vite build --ssr src/entry-server.js # SSR build

# Preview production build
vite preview [root]
vite preview --port 8080
```

## Configuration File (vite.config.js)

Define Vite configuration using the `defineConfig` helper for type hints and validation.

```javascript
// vite.config.js
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import vue from '@vitejs/plugin-vue'
import path from 'path'

export default defineConfig({
  // Project root directory
  root: process.cwd(),

  // Base public path
  base: '/',

  // Mode: 'development' | 'production'
  mode: 'development',

  // Plugins array
  plugins: [vue()],

  // Public directory for static assets
  publicDir: 'public',

  // Cache directory
  cacheDir: 'node_modules/.vite',

  // Resolve options
  resolve: {
    alias: {
      '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src'),
      'utils': path.resolve(__dirname, './src/utils'),
    },
    extensions: ['.mjs', '.js', '.ts', '.jsx', '.tsx', '.json'],
  },

  // Global constant replacements
  define: {
    __APP_VERSION__: JSON.stringify('1.0.0'),
    __API_URL__: JSON.stringify('https://api.example.com'),
  },

  // CSS options
  css: {
    modules: {
      localsConvention: 'camelCase',
    },
    preprocessorOptions: {
      scss: {
        additionalData: `@import "@/styles/variables.scss";`,
      },
    },
  },

  // JSON handling
  json: {
    namedExports: true,
    stringify: 'auto',
  },

  // Environment variables
  envDir: './',
  envPrefix: 'VITE_',

  // Logging
  logLevel: 'info',
  clearScreen: true,
})
```

## Server Options

Configure the development server for local development with HMR, proxying, and CORS support.

```javascript
// vite.config.js
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'

export default defineConfig({
  server: {
    host: '0.0.0.0',           // Listen on all interfaces
    port: 3000,                 // Dev server port
    strictPort: true,           // Exit if port is in use
    open: true,                 // Open browser on startup
    cors: true,                 // Enable CORS

    // Proxy configuration for API requests
    proxy: {
      '/api': {
        target: 'http://localhost:8080',
        changeOrigin: true,
        rewrite: (path) => path.replace(/^\/api/, ''),
      },
      '/socket.io': {
        target: 'ws://localhost:8080',
        ws: true,
      },
    },

    // File system access restrictions
    fs: {
      strict: true,
      allow: ['..'],
    },

    // HMR configuration
    hmr: {
      overlay: true,
      port: 3001,
    },

    // Watch options
    watch: {
      usePolling: true,         // For Docker/WSL
      interval: 100,
    },
  },
})
```

## Build Options

Configure the production build with code splitting, minification, and output options.

```javascript
// vite.config.js
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'

export default defineConfig({
  build: {
    target: 'es2015',           // Browser compatibility target
    outDir: 'dist',             // Output directory
    assetsDir: 'assets',        // Assets subdirectory
    assetsInlineLimit: 4096,    // Inline assets smaller than 4kb
    cssCodeSplit: true,         // Enable CSS code splitting
    sourcemap: true,            // Generate source maps: true | 'inline' | 'hidden'
    minify: 'oxc',              // Minifier: 'oxc' | 'terser' | 'esbuild' | false

    // Rolldown options
    rolldownOptions: {
      input: {
        main: 'index.html',
        nested: 'nested/index.html',
      },
      output: {
        manualChunks: {
          vendor: ['vue', 'vue-router'],
        },
        entryFileNames: 'js/[name]-[hash].js',
        chunkFileNames: 'js/[name]-[hash].js',
        assetFileNames: 'assets/[name]-[hash][extname]',
      },
    },

    // Library mode
    lib: {
      entry: 'src/lib/main.js',
      name: 'MyLib',
      fileName: (format) => `my-lib.${format}.js`,
      formats: ['es', 'umd'],
    },

    // SSR build
    ssr: 'src/entry-server.js',

    // Generate manifest
    manifest: true,
    ssrManifest: true,

    // License generation
    license: true,

    // Performance
    chunkSizeWarningLimit: 500,
    reportCompressedSize: true,
    emptyOutDir: true,
  },
})
```

## JavaScript API - createServer

Create and configure a Vite dev server programmatically for custom integrations and middleware mode.

```javascript
import { createServer } from 'vite'

// Basic usage
const server = await createServer({
  configFile: false,
  root: process.cwd(),
  server: {
    port: 1337,
  },
})

await server.listen()
server.printUrls()
server.bindCLIShortcuts({ print: true })

// Middleware mode for Express integration
import express from 'express'

const app = express()

const vite = await createServer({
  server: {
    middlewareMode: true,
  },
  appType: 'custom',
})

// Use Vite's middlewares
app.use(vite.middlewares)

app.use('*', async (req, res, next) => {
  const url = req.originalUrl

  try {
    // Read index.html
    let template = fs.readFileSync('index.html', 'utf-8')

    // Apply Vite HTML transforms
    template = await vite.transformIndexHtml(url, template)

    // Load server entry
    const { render } = await vite.ssrLoadModule('/src/entry-server.js')

    // Render the app
    const appHtml = await render(url)

    const html = template.replace('<!--app-html-->', appHtml)

    res.status(200).set({ 'Content-Type': 'text/html' }).end(html)
  } catch (e) {
    vite.ssrFixStacktrace(e)
    next(e)
  }
})

app.listen(5173)
```

## JavaScript API - build

Build for production programmatically with full control over the build process.

```javascript
import { build } from 'vite'
import path from 'path'

// Basic build
await build({
  root: path.resolve(__dirname, './project'),
  base: '/foo/',
  build: {
    outDir: 'dist',
    rolldownOptions: {
      input: {
        main: 'index.html',
      },
    },
  },
})

// Library build
await build({
  build: {
    lib: {
      entry: path.resolve(__dirname, 'lib/main.js'),
      name: 'MyLibrary',
      fileName: 'my-library',
      formats: ['es', 'cjs', 'umd'],
    },
    rolldownOptions: {
      external: ['vue'],
      output: {
        globals: {
          vue: 'Vue',
        },
      },
    },
  },
})

// Multiple builds
const configs = [
  { build: { outDir: 'dist/esm', lib: { formats: ['es'] } } },
  { build: { outDir: 'dist/cjs', lib: { formats: ['cjs'] } } },
]

for (const config of configs) {
  await build(config)
}
```

## JavaScript API - preview

Preview production build locally with the same server configuration as development.

```javascript
import { preview } from 'vite'

const previewServer = await preview({
  preview: {
    port: 8080,
    open: true,
    host: true,
  },
})

previewServer.printUrls()
previewServer.bindCLIShortcuts({ print: true })
```

## JavaScript API - Utility Functions

Vite exports utility functions for configuration management and environment handling.

```javascript
import {
  defineConfig,
  loadEnv,
  mergeConfig,
  resolveConfig,
  searchForWorkspaceRoot,
  normalizePath,
  transformWithOxc,
  preprocessCSS,
  loadConfigFromFile,
  version,
  rolldownVersion,
} from 'vite'

// Load environment variables
const env = loadEnv('production', process.cwd(), 'VITE_')
console.log(env.VITE_API_URL)

// Merge configurations
const baseConfig = { base: '/' }
const envConfig = { define: { __DEV__: 'false' } }
const merged = mergeConfig(baseConfig, envConfig)

// Resolve full config
const resolved = await resolveConfig({}, 'build', 'production')

// Find workspace root
const workspaceRoot = searchForWorkspaceRoot(process.cwd())

// Normalize paths for cross-platform compatibility
const normalized = normalizePath('src\\components\\App.vue')
// Returns: 'src/components/App.vue'

// Transform TypeScript/JSX with Oxc
const result = await transformWithOxc(
  `const x: number = 1`,
  'file.ts',
  { target: 'es2020' }
)

// Preprocess CSS
const cssResult = await preprocessCSS(
  '$color: red; .btn { color: $color; }',
  'styles.scss',
  resolved
)

// Load config from file
const { config, path: configPath } = await loadConfigFromFile(
  { command: 'serve', mode: 'development' },
  'vite.config.js'
)

// Check versions
console.log(`Vite: ${version}`)
console.log(`Rolldown: ${rolldownVersion}`)
```

## Plugin API - Basic Plugin Structure

Create Vite plugins to extend functionality with hooks for resolving, loading, and transforming modules.

```javascript
// vite-plugin-example.js
export default function myPlugin(options = {}) {
  return {
    name: 'vite-plugin-example',

    // Plugin ordering: 'pre' | 'post' | undefined
    enforce: 'pre',

    // Apply only to serve or build
    apply: 'serve', // 'serve' | 'build' | function

    // Modify Vite config before resolution
    config(config, { command, mode }) {
      return {
        resolve: {
          alias: {
            '@utils': '/src/utils',
          },
        },
      }
    },

    // Access resolved config
    configResolved(resolvedConfig) {
      console.log('Root:', resolvedConfig.root)
      console.log('Mode:', resolvedConfig.mode)
    },

    // Resolve module IDs
    resolveId: {
      filter: { id: /^virtual:/ },
      handler(id) {
        if (id === 'virtual:my-module') {
          return '\0virtual:my-module'
        }
      },
    },

    // Load virtual modules
    load: {
      filter: { id: /^\0virtual:/ },
      handler(id) {
        if (id === '\0virtual:my-module') {
          return `export const msg = "Hello from virtual module"`
        }
      },
    },

    // Transform module content
    transform: {
      filter: { id: /\.my-ext$/ },
      handler(code, id) {
        return {
          code: transformCode(code),
          map: null,
        }
      },
    },
  }
}

// Usage in vite.config.js
import myPlugin from './vite-plugin-example'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [myPlugin({ option: 'value' })],
})
```

## Plugin API - Server Hooks

Configure the dev server with custom middlewares and WebSocket handling.

```javascript
// vite-plugin-server.js
export default function serverPlugin() {
  let server

  return {
    name: 'vite-plugin-server',

    // Configure dev server
    configureServer(_server) {
      server = _server

      // Add middleware before Vite's middlewares
      server.middlewares.use((req, res, next) => {
        if (req.url === '/api/custom') {
          res.end(JSON.stringify({ custom: 'response' }))
          return
        }
        next()
      })

      // Return function to add middleware after Vite's middlewares
      return () => {
        server.middlewares.use((req, res, next) => {
          // Post middleware logic
          next()
        })
      }
    },

    // Configure preview server
    configurePreviewServer(server) {
      return () => {
        server.middlewares.use((req, res, next) => {
          // Preview server middleware
          next()
        })
      }
    },

    // Handle HMR updates
    handleHotUpdate({ file, server, modules, timestamp, read }) {
      if (file.endsWith('.custom')) {
        // Custom HMR handling
        server.ws.send({
          type: 'custom',
          event: 'custom-update',
          data: { file },
        })
        return [] // Prevent default HMR
      }

      // Filter affected modules
      return modules.filter((mod) => !mod.id?.includes('node_modules'))
    },
  }
}
```

## Plugin API - HTML Transform

Transform HTML entry files with tag injection and content manipulation.

```javascript
// vite-plugin-html.js
export default function htmlPlugin() {
  return {
    name: 'vite-plugin-html',

    transformIndexHtml: {
      order: 'pre', // 'pre' | 'post' | undefined

      handler(html, ctx) {
        // ctx.path - URL path
        // ctx.filename - file path
        // ctx.server - ViteDevServer (dev only)
        // ctx.bundle - Rollup output bundle (build only)

        // Return transformed HTML string
        return html.replace(
          /<title>(.*?)<\/title>/,
          '<title>My Custom Title</title>'
        )

        // Or return tag descriptors
        return {
          html,
          tags: [
            {
              tag: 'script',
              attrs: { src: '/analytics.js' },
              injectTo: 'head',
            },
            {
              tag: 'meta',
              attrs: {
                name: 'description',
                content: 'My app description',
              },
              injectTo: 'head',
            },
            {
              tag: 'div',
              attrs: { id: 'app-version' },
              children: 'v1.0.0',
              injectTo: 'body-prepend',
            },
          ],
        }
      },
    },
  }
}
```

## HMR API

Use the HMR API in client code to handle hot module updates for custom file types.

```javascript
// Counter module with HMR support
export let count = 0

export function increment() {
  count++
  render()
}

function render() {
  document.getElementById('count').textContent = count
}

render()

// HMR handling
if (import.meta.hot) {
  // Accept self updates
  import.meta.hot.accept((newModule) => {
    if (newModule) {
      console.log('Module updated:', newModule.count)
    }
  })

  // Accept dependency updates
  import.meta.hot.accept('./dep.js', (newDep) => {
    if (newDep) {
      newDep.init()
    }
  })

  // Accept multiple dependencies
  import.meta.hot.accept(
    ['./foo.js', './bar.js'],
    ([newFoo, newBar]) => {
      // Handle updates
    }
  )

  // Cleanup on module dispose
  import.meta.hot.dispose((data) => {
    // Save state to data for next module instance
    data.count = count
    // Cleanup side effects
    clearInterval(timer)
  })

  // Restore state from previous module
  if (import.meta.hot.data.count) {
    count = import.meta.hot.data.count
    render()
  }

  // Cleanup when module is no longer imported
  import.meta.hot.prune(() => {
    removeGlobalEventListeners()
  })

  // Invalidate module and propagate to importers
  import.meta.hot.invalidate('Cannot handle this update')

  // Listen to HMR events
  import.meta.hot.on('vite:beforeUpdate', (payload) => {
    console.log('About to update:', payload)
  })

  import.meta.hot.on('vite:error', (error) => {
    console.error('HMR error:', error)
  })

  // Custom events from plugins
  import.meta.hot.on('my-plugin:update', (data) => {
    console.log('Custom update:', data)
  })

  // Send events to server
  import.meta.hot.send('my-plugin:from-client', { message: 'Hello' })
}
```

## Environment Variables

Access and configure environment variables with type safety and mode-specific values.

```bash
# .env - loaded in all cases
VITE_APP_TITLE=My App
VITE_API_URL=https://api.example.com

# .env.local - loaded in all cases, ignored by git
VITE_LOCAL_SECRET=local-only-value

# .env.development - only loaded in development mode
VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:3000/api
VITE_DEBUG=true

# .env.production - only loaded in production mode
VITE_API_URL=https://api.production.com
VITE_ANALYTICS_ID=UA-123456

# .env.staging - custom mode
VITE_API_URL=https://api.staging.com
```

```javascript
// Access environment variables in code
console.log(import.meta.env.VITE_APP_TITLE)  // "My App"
console.log(import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL)    // URL based on mode
console.log(import.meta.env.MODE)             // "development" | "production" | custom
console.log(import.meta.env.DEV)              // true in dev
console.log(import.meta.env.PROD)             // true in production
console.log(import.meta.env.SSR)              // true in SSR

// Type definitions for custom env variables (vite-env.d.ts)
interface ImportMetaEnv {
  readonly VITE_APP_TITLE: string
  readonly VITE_API_URL: string
  readonly VITE_DEBUG?: string
}

interface ImportMeta {
  readonly env: ImportMetaEnv
}
```

```html
<!-- HTML constant replacement -->
<title>%VITE_APP_TITLE%</title>
<p>Running in %MODE% mode</p>
```

## Static Asset Handling

Import and reference static assets with special query parameters for different use cases.

```javascript
// Import as URL (resolved path)
import imgUrl from './img.png'
document.getElementById('hero').src = imgUrl

// Import as string content
import shaderCode from './shader.glsl?raw'
console.log(shaderCode)

// Import as explicit URL
import workerUrl from './worker.js?url'

// Import JSON with named exports
import { version, name } from './package.json'
console.log(`${name}@${version}`)

// Import entire JSON object
import pkg from './package.json'
console.log(pkg.dependencies)

// Web Workers
import MyWorker from './worker.js?worker'
const worker = new MyWorker()

// Inline worker as base64
import InlineWorker from './worker.js?worker&inline'

// Standard worker constructor (recommended)
const worker = new Worker(new URL('./worker.js', import.meta.url), {
  type: 'module',
})

// WebAssembly
import init from './module.wasm?init'
init().then((instance) => {
  instance.exports.myFunction()
})
```

## Glob Import

Dynamically import multiple modules matching glob patterns.

```javascript
// Lazy load all modules matching pattern
const modules = import.meta.glob('./modules/*.js')
// { './modules/a.js': () => import('./modules/a.js'), ... }

for (const path in modules) {
  modules[path]().then((mod) => {
    console.log(path, mod.default)
  })
}

// Eager loading (no dynamic import)
const eagerModules = import.meta.glob('./modules/*.js', { eager: true })
// { './modules/a.js': { default: ... }, ... }

// Named imports only
const setups = import.meta.glob('./modules/*.js', { import: 'setup' })

// Default export with eager loading
const defaults = import.meta.glob('./modules/*.js', {
  import: 'default',
  eager: true,
})

// Multiple patterns
const all = import.meta.glob(['./modules/*.js', './other/*.ts'])

// Negative patterns (exclude)
const filtered = import.meta.glob(['./modules/*.js', '!**/excluded.js'])

// Custom queries
const rawFiles = import.meta.glob('./assets/*.svg', {
  query: '?raw',
  import: 'default',
})

const urlFiles = import.meta.glob('./assets/*.svg', {
  query: '?url',
  import: 'default',
})
```

## CSS Features

Import and use CSS with modules, preprocessors, and special features.

```javascript
// Regular CSS import (injected to page)
import './styles.css'

// CSS Modules
import styles from './component.module.css'
element.className = styles.container

// Named imports with camelCase conversion
import { buttonPrimary, cardHeader } from './styles.module.css'

// Disable injection, get CSS string
import cssText from './styles.css?inline'

// CSS preprocessors (requires preprocessor installation)
import './styles.scss'
import './styles.less'
import './styles.styl'
```

```css
/* component.module.css */
.container {
  padding: 1rem;
}

.button-primary {
  background: blue;
}
```

```javascript
// vite.config.js - CSS configuration
export default defineConfig({
  css: {
    modules: {
      localsConvention: 'camelCaseOnly',
      generateScopedName: '[name]__[local]___[hash:base64:5]',
    },
    preprocessorOptions: {
      scss: {
        additionalData: `@import "@/styles/variables.scss";`,
      },
      less: {
        math: 'parens-division',
      },
    },
    // Use Lightning CSS for processing
    transformer: 'lightningcss',
    lightningcss: {
      targets: { chrome: 111 },
    },
  },
})
```

## TypeScript Configuration

Configure TypeScript with Vite using proper compiler options and type definitions.

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "useDefineForClassFields": true,
    "module": "ESNext",
    "lib": ["ES2020", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"],
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "allowImportingTsExtensions": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "strict": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true,
    "types": ["vite/client"]
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

```typescript
// vite-env.d.ts - Type declarations
/// <reference types="vite/client" />

// Custom asset type declarations
declare module '*.svg' {
  const content: string
  export default content
}

declare module '*.glsl' {
  const content: string
  export default content
}

// Environment variable types
interface ImportMetaEnv {
  readonly VITE_APP_TITLE: string
  readonly VITE_API_URL: string
}

interface ImportMeta {
  readonly env: ImportMetaEnv
}
```

## Server-Side Rendering (SSR)

Configure Vite for server-side rendering with entry points for client and server.

```javascript
// vite.config.js
export default defineConfig({
  build: {
    ssr: true,
    rolldownOptions: {
      input: 'src/entry-server.js',
    },
  },
  ssr: {
    // Externalize dependencies for SSR
    external: ['express'],
    noExternal: ['my-lib-with-css'],
  },
})
```

```javascript
// entry-server.js
import { renderToString } from 'react-dom/server'
import App from './App'

export async function render(url) {
  const html = renderToString(<App url={url} />)
  return html
}
```

```javascript
// server.js - Production SSR server
import express from 'express'
import { readFileSync } from 'fs'

const app = express()

// Serve static assets
app.use('/assets', express.static('dist/client/assets'))

// SSR handler
app.use('*', async (req, res) => {
  const template = readFileSync('dist/client/index.html', 'utf-8')
  const { render } = await import('./dist/server/entry-server.js')

  const appHtml = await render(req.originalUrl)
  const html = template.replace('<!--app-html-->', appHtml)

  res.status(200).set({ 'Content-Type': 'text/html' }).end(html)
})

app.listen(3000)
```

Vite excels at rapid development with instant HMR updates, making it ideal for modern frontend frameworks like Vue, React, and Svelte. The tool handles complex build scenarios including library mode for npm packages, SSR applications, and multi-page applications. Its plugin system supports extending functionality for custom file types, build transforms, and development server enhancements.

For production builds, Vite leverages Rolldown bundling with automatic code splitting, CSS extraction, and asset optimization. The configuration is minimal by default but highly customizable for enterprise requirements including custom output structures, external dependencies, and environment-specific builds. Integration with backend frameworks is straightforward through middleware mode and manifest generation for asset path resolution.
